currentFAQs.q: How much does a concrete driveway cost in {{CITY}}?
currentFAQs.a: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for a concrete driveway, depending on size and finish type.

currentFAQs.q: Is stamped concrete worth the extra cost?
currentFAQs.a: Stamped concrete costs 30-50% more than plain concrete but can mimic the look of pavers, brick, or natural stone at a fraction of the cost. It adds significant curb appeal and value.

currentFAQs.q: How long does a concrete driveway last?
currentFAQs.a: A properly installed and sealed concrete driveway typically lasts 25-30 years. Regular sealing every 2-3 years and prompt crack repair can extend its lifespan further.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: How much does a concrete driveway cost in {{CITY}}?
Why is concrete more | less expensive in {{CITY}}?
What concrete mix handles {{CITY}}'s freeze-thaw cycle?
proposedFAQs.note: Air-entrained concrete is needed in freeze-thaw zones; not needed in mild climates. Strong climate-distinct signal.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: When can concrete be poured in {{CITY}}?
proposedFAQs.note: Concrete has hard temp constraints (40-90°F ideal). seasonNote will give honest per-city answers.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: What red flags should I watch for hiring a concrete contractor in {{CITY}}?
Do I need a permit for concrete work in {{CITY}}, {{STATE}}?
proposedFAQs.note: Driveway permits vary by municipality but state code sets the baseline (typically required for area > some sqft or any street curb cut).
